goal:
Fix 4 high-severity audit findings. DO NOT add new features, only fix the issues described:

1. SUPERVISOR prompt injection: In internal/orchestrator/service.go, wherever worker/system summaries or block reasons are written to job.LeaderContextSummary, strip any text that starts with "[SUPERVISOR]" from the untrusted input. Add a helper sanitizeLeaderContext(text string) string that removes lines starting with "[SUPERVISOR]". Apply it in runWorkerStep and runSystemStep when setting LeaderContextSummary.

2. Workspace validation strengthening: In both internal/orchestrator/service.go validateWorkspaceDir and internal/mcp/server.go validateWorkspaceDir, add: (a) require absolute path using filepath.IsAbs, (b) resolve symlinks with filepath.EvalSymlinks before stat check. Unify the two copies into one shared function if possible (put in a new file internal/orchestrator/workspace.go and import from mcp).

3. Steer authorization: In internal/orchestrator/service.go Steer method, check that job.Status is "running" or "waiting_leader" or "waiting_worker" before accepting the directive. Return error for done/failed/cancelled jobs.

4. Steer directive preservation: In internal/orchestrator/service.go, instead of writing to LeaderContextSummary directly, add a new field SupervisorDirective string to domain.Job (in internal/domain/types.go). Steer writes to this field. In internal/provider/protocol.go buildLeaderPrompt, if job.SupervisorDirective is non-empty, include it as a separate section BEFORE the job state. After the leader processes it (when runLoop gets the next leader response), clear the field.

Run go build ./... and go test ./... to verify.
